messages.author: Alec K2XAP
messages.text: Hi! Have you found a regional discord group yet? I know there are folks in Albany and Rochester, and I’m sure some in between. BuffaLoRa is also out there but not sure how much MeshCore they’re running. Btw we have some folks from all over the area here: https://discord.nyme.sh and can help connect. Mostly Meshtastic activity but some MeshCore discussion.

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.text: I'm new to Meshcore in Syracuse. We have a lot of Meshtastic users, but only a handful of Meshcore and I have yet to connect with anyone outside of my repeater and two nodes. Meshcore seems to be robust from my limited testing.
messages.content_html: I'm new to Meshcore in Syracuse. We have a lot of Meshtastic users, but only a handful of Meshcore and I have yet to connect with anyone outside of my repeater and two nodes. Meshcore seems to be robust from my limited testing.

messages.author: Jay KE2BYT
messages.text: Same here in the Buffalo area. I think we are just a little ahead of the curve. Meshcore seems to be replacing Meshtastic around the globe. Meshcore definitely makes more sense in rural areas which is why I decided to focus on it instead of Meshtastic.
messages.content_html: Same here in the Buffalo area. I think we are just a little ahead of the curve. Meshcore seems to be replacing Meshtastic around the globe. Meshcore definitely makes more sense in rural areas which is why I decided to focus on it instead of Meshtastic.

messages.author: Alec K2XAP
messages.text: Ultimately it just depends on what the community you want to connect with is using. They aren't mutually exclusive and both can work at scale (with different degrees of difficulty). Many communities are switching to MeshCore but some are sticking with Meshtastic, or doing both in parallel. Easy enough to run both! (edited)
messages.content_html: Ultimately it just depends on what the community you want to connect with is using. They aren't mutually exclusive and both can work at scale (with different degrees of difficulty). Many communities are switching to MeshCore but some are sticking with Meshtastic, or doing both in parallel. Easy enough to run both! (edited)

messages.author: Jay KE2BYT
messages.text: True, but I think that the repeater/client model of meshcore and flood routing makes more sense for rural areas. I am far enough away from the nearest meshtastic node where I am basically doing my own thing anyways.
messages.content_html: True, but I think that the repeater/client model of meshcore and flood routing makes more sense for rural areas. I am far enough away from the nearest meshtastic node where I am basically doing my own thing anyways.

messages.text: Wheatfield, here. Never even heard of Meshcore before last week, but am looking to dive in. Rather involved with Meshtastic, but don't see why it'd ever have to be a "Chevy or Ford" thing; I'm going to buy a couple nodes, flash them with Meshcore, & talk to absolutely nobody on that too, LoL.

messages.text: Boston, Ny. Will be doing my best to build up MeshCore in WNY. Unfortunately I live deep in the valley and can not even hit other meshcore devices, so it will be a steep hill!! (edited)

messages.author: Tentoa
messages.text: @Sid-NY Well from what I've read of it so far, you should be able to make contact with my node in Wheatfield, whenever I get around to it. Do you already have the hardware (& are you already on Meshtastic)? You're in a good position there to contribute to both systems, if you're willing....if not, I'd still love to break local ground with 'core' here alone, if you're interested

messages.author: Sid-NY
messages.text: Nice! I run both Meshtastic and MeshCore, but stay away from mqtt. I’ll be your core builder partner, for sure. I defiantly see core as being better for WNY eventually. I have several nodes in parts, and on shelves waiting to be deployed. For now I need to figure out how to get out of the Boston valley, on any lora mode, and need to make some friends with someone on the western slope. Today I had the most pathetic meshtastic range test possible driving around. Hopefully once I get mounted on the roof I can get coverage… Let me know when you have your Wheatfield node in place.

messages.author: Tentoa
messages.text: I definitely will, but I expect by May. I have a Mesh node that I travel with, & even though I pick up other nodes, I've yet to make contact with anyone. Seems no one really treats them as a means of communication, LoL. I've recently started attending the BuffaLoRa meet-ups; it's actually a right good lot of people, but....all conversation is here on discord, LoLoL. It's like a bunch of wine enthusiasts discussing their love if wine over beers. 99% of the messages I receive on ANY of my nodes are "test" & "ping." If the weather is bad, maybe a "be safe out there," & "happy...." whatever, if it's a holiday. I just think it's hilarious that the focus is on a communication technology, yet ALL communication is done online...
messages.content_html: I definitely will, but I expect by May. I have a Mesh node that I travel with, & even though I pick up other nodes, I've yet to make contact with anyone. I just think it's hilarious that the focus is on a communication technology, yet ALL communication is done online...

messages.author: Sid-NY
messages.text: I hear you!!! I find that the same can be said of many of the ham bands as well. I actually struggle with a what’s the point now and again… Fundamentally there are much better forms of communication (such as discord) and ignoring them for an inferior form to me makes no sense. So I likely will be more conversational here than on Lora. For me, often the motivation for my interest revolves around my interest in electronics, and tinkering.

messages.author: Sid-NY
messages.text: Welcome to the official communication channels for our Western New York MeshCore network! These configurations are specific to the MeshCore infrastructure we are deploying across the 716 area. Channel 0: The Public Backbone Name: Public Key (PSK): 8b3387e9c5cdea6ac9e5edbaa115cd72 Purpose: The default public channel . By keeping this active, you help route traffic and build the foundational mesh for the entire Western New York region. Be a good citizen of the mesh and leave this on! ### Channel 1: The Community Comms (Private) Name: Meshcore716 Key (PSK): 096a7faa51e9076040a9d4175ec53afc Purpose: Our encrypted, community-only channel. We use this space to test line-of-sight, coordinate local hardware builds, and chat without spamming the public airwaves. ### Channel 2: Regional General & Alerts Name: #wny Key (PSK): ad145f5860c081d04dadd28f7a0b3cde Purpose: A dedicated channel for WNY-wide announcements, severe weather alerts, or general local routing. (edited)
messages.content_html:
Welcome to the official communication channels for our Western New York MeshCore network! These configurations are specific to the MeshCore infrastructure we are deploying across the 716 area.

Channel 0: The Public Backbone

  • Name: Public
  • Key (PSK): 8b3387e9c5cdea6ac9e5edbaa115cd72
  • Purpose: The default public channel . By keeping this active, you help route traffic and build the foundational mesh for the entire Western New York region. Be a good citizen of the mesh and leave this on! ### Channel 1: The Community Comms (Private)
  • Name: Meshcore716
  • Key (PSK): 096a7faa51e9076040a9d4175ec53afc
  • Purpose: Our encrypted, community-only channel. We use this space to test line-of-sight, coordinate local hardware builds, and chat without spamming the public airwaves. ### Channel 2: Regional General & Alerts
  • Name: #wny
  • Key (PSK): ad145f5860c081d04dadd28f7a0b3cde
  • Purpose: A dedicated channel for WNY-wide announcements, severe weather alerts, or general local routing.
 (edited)

messages.author: Tentoa
messages.text: @Sid-NY hey man. I'M SORRY, I thought I was going to be able to make it to that meeting; I'm sorry I didn't at least mention that I wasn't able to make it. I work for a company called Linde, & while I usually only average ~50ish hours a week over 5 days, I've been cranking out close to 70 over 6. No excuse for completely falling off, but that's the situation... I'm located in Wheatfield, just passed the North Tonawanda border. This area is fairly built up with meshtastic, but I'm going to be adding both a 'tastic AND a 'core node to my chimney this spring, so hopefully it'll give a solid boost. Roughly where in the southern tier are you? I'm down that way all the time (in fact, I'll be heading to Warren, PA in about 20 minutes from now). Just wanted to reach out, & apologize for my sudden absence. Hope to speak with you soon, & that the next meeting isn't a bust...take care, man.

Just wanted to reach out, & apologize for my sudden absence. Hope to speak with you soon, & that the next meeting isn't a bust...take care, man.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
